title: Is Innodata Well-Positioned for America's Accelerating AI Spend?
text: The article analyzes Innodata Inc. (INOD) as a strategic beneficiary of expanding U.S. artificial intelligence investments. The company operates as a picks-and-shovels provider to major AI developers, delivering record Q3 2025 revenues of $62.6 million a 20% year-over-year increase with adjusted EBITDA margins of 26%. Key growth drivers include: Strong relationships with major Big Tech foundation model developers. Projected 45%+ organic revenue growth for 2025. Contracts potentially generating approximately $68 million in future revenues. Launch of Innodata Federal targeting U.S. defense and civilian agencies, with an anticipated $25 million initial project. The company maintains $73.9 million in cash with no debt, providing operational flexibility. Competitors include Globant SA and Cognizant Technology Solutions which represent relevant competitors in AI services and data engineering. INOD trades at a forward P/E ratio of 44.33 versus the industry average of 16.99. The Zacks consensus estimates show 2025 earnings of 89 cents and 2026 earnings of $1.20. The stock carries a Zacks Rank #3 (Hold) rating.
